prompt: Repository: /home/skogix/.local/src/gptme-contrib. User requested autonomous team coordination. Read-only assessment only. Identify the repository's actual work queue and current highest-priority actionable items. Inspect local routing/instruction files first, then issue/task/TODO/planning files and current branch status. Do not modify anything. Preserve the user's 8 uncommitted files. Return: (1) exact queue sources and paths, (2) ranked actionable tasks with evidence, (3) whether any task is sufficiently specified for implementation, (4) blockers.

Repository: /home/skogix/.local/src/gptme-contrib. User requested autonomous team coordination. Read-only assessment only. Audit current git state and collaboration signals: branch/upstream divergence, uncommitted paths and their apparent purpose, recent commits, configured remotes, and any local notification/inbox artifacts or GitHub issues/PRs if safely accessible. Do not edit, stage, commit, or push. Return concise evidence and warn about overlap risks for delegated changes.

Using the current thread context and the commit and pull request contexts below, generate one git commit message plus one pull request title and body.
Write the result into the structured response fields message, title, and body.
message must contain plain commit-message text only, not JSON, field labels, markdown, or code fences.
Make 0 tool calls.
If context includes pull request instructions, follow them even when they conflict with the default pull request rules below.
Commit message rules:
- Use an imperative verb first.
- Keep message under 72 characters.
- Do not add a scope prefix unless the context already clearly uses one.
- Do not include markdown, quotes, or trailing punctuation.
- If context includes custom commit instructions, apply them to message only.
Pull request language rules:
- For the PR title and body, match the primary language of the current thread or user request.
- Do not translate a non-English thread to English by default.
- Translate standard section headings such as Summary and Testing when writing in another language.
Fallback PR title rules:
- title must contain only the PR title, not JSON, field labels, or body content.
- Use an imperative or action-oriented phrasing first.
- Keep title under 120 characters.
- No trailing punctuation.
Pull request body rules:
- body must contain only the PR body, not JSON, field labels, the title, or a full PR draft.
- Do not repeat, restate, or label the title inside body.
- Keep the body concise and scannable.
- Use Markdown with short bullets.
- Include a Summary section and a Testing section.
- In Testing, describe meaningful validation at a high level, such as new unit or integration tests, or local UI testing with Playwright.
- Do not paste command transcripts. For routine checks, summarize the result, for example: lint and formatting passed.
- If tests were not run, say "Not run (not requested)".
- If context includes pull request instructions, apply them to title/body only.
